prose: **Aniline Fukui reactivity analysis** — DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density fitting on), PySCF (version as provided by chemkit), gas phase; charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz`, single-point calculation (no geometry optimization). Partial charges: Mulliken (PySCF). SCF converged; no warnings reported. CLI invocation: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqgo27r4k/live.json`. Results: the most electrophilic site is C3 (atom label 3) with Fukui f⁺ = 2.2975867; the most nucleophilic site is N (atom label 1) with Fukui f⁻ = 0.20452359. The full array of f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, and dual descriptors is available in the JSON output and as a plot (`.../agent_call_01_plot.png`).
